 {"id":"beads_rust-b9o","title":"Routing + redirects (routes.jsonl + redirect file)","description":"# Routing + Redirects (routes.jsonl)

## Purpose
Implement classic cross-repo routing used by `show`, `update`, `close`, etc. This is **not** git automation; it only resolves which `.beads` directory to open for a given ID prefix.

## Key Artifacts
- `.beads/routes.jsonl` entries: `{ "prefix": "bd-", "path": "beads/mayor/rig" }`
- Town root: detected via `mayor/town.json` when walking up.
- `.beads/redirect`: overrides target beads dir for local setups.

## Resolution Rules
- Extract prefix from ID (substring before first `-`, plus hyphen).
- Search order:
  1) local `.beads/routes.jsonl`
  2) town root `.beads/routes.jsonl`
- If route found:
  - `path == "."` => town-level `.beads`
  - else resolve relative to town root
- If `.beads/redirect` exists in target, follow it (relative to current beads dir).
- If target missing/invalid, command errors.

## External Ref Derivation
- If prefix matches a route, it can map to `external:<project>:<id>` for external dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prefix routing matches classic order and redirect behavior.
- Commands open routed DBs read-only or read/write as needed.

## Tests
- Route resolution with local + town root routes.jsonl.
- Redirect file override.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ClaudeOpus","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:16.726187846Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:01:18.790181849Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:01:18.790135021Z","close_reason":"Implemented routing module with routes.jsonl parsing, redirect file handling, town root detection, and comprehensive tests.  {"id":"beads_rust-biw","title":"search Command Implementation (FTS5)","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br search` command for full-text search across issues using SQLite FTS5 (Full-Text Search 5). This enables fast, relevance-ranked searches across issue titles, descriptions, and comments.

## CLI Interface
```
br search <query> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <query>                   Search query (supports FTS5 syntax)

Options:
  -t, --type <TYPE>         Filter by issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
  -s, --status <STATUS>     Filter by status (open, closed, in_progress, etc.)
  -p, --priority <PRIORITY> Filter by priority (0-4)
  -l, --label <LABEL>       Filter by label (can be repeated)
  --limit <N>               Maximum results (default: 50)
  --offset <N>              Skip first N results
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Technical Requirements

### FTS5 Virtual Table
```sql
-- Create FTS5 table for search
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    title,
    description,
    -- Use porter stemmer for English
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;
```

### Search Implementation
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn search_issues(&self, query: &str, filter: &SearchFilter) -> Result<Vec<IssueWithScore>> {
        // FTS5 query with BM25 ranking
        let sql = r#"
            SELECT 
                i.*,
                bm25(issues_fts) as score
            FROM issues i
            JOIN issues_fts f ON i.id = f.id
            WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?1
              AND (?2 IS NULL OR i.status = ?2)
              AND (?3 IS NULL OR i.issue_type = ?3)
              AND (?4 IS NULL OR i.priority = ?4)
            ORDER BY score
            LIMIT ?5 OFFSET ?6
        "#;
        
        // ... execute query
    }
    
    pub fn rebuild_fts_index(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        // For initial population or repair
        self.conn.execute("DELETE FROM issues_fts", [])?;
        self.conn.execute(
            "INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description) 
             SELECT id, title, description FROM issues",
            []
        )?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### FTS5 Query Syntax Support
Users can use FTS5 extended query syntax:
- Simple terms: `authentication bug`
- Phrase search: `"login failed"`
- Prefix search: `auth*`
- Boolean operators: `auth AND NOT password`
- Column filters: `title:authentication`
- NEAR operator: `NEAR(login password, 5)`

### Comments Search (Optional)
```sql
-- Separate FTS table for comments
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    issue_id,
    content,
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61'
);
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Search results for "authentication bug":

1. [bd-abc12] [BUG] P0 Authentication fails silently
   ...the authentication system throws no error when...
   Score: 0.95

2. [bd-def34] [FEATURE] P2 Add OAuth authentication
   ...implement OAuth authentication for external...
   Score: 0.72

Found 2 matches (50ms)
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "query": "authentication bug",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "bd-abc12",
      "title": "Authentication fails silently",
      "issue_type": "bug",
      "priority": 0,
      "score": 0.95,
      "snippet": "...the authentication system throws no error when..."
    }
  ],
  "total": 2,
  "elapsed_ms": 50
}
```

## Snippet Generation
Generate contextual snippets highlighting matching terms:
```rust
fn generate_snippet(text: &str, query: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {
    // Use FTS5 snippet() function or implement custom
    // Highlight with ANSI codes for terminal
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FTS5 virtual table created during schema migration
- [ ] Triggers maintain FTS index on CRUD operations
- [ ] Search by title and description
- [ ] BM25 relevance ranking
- [ ] Filters combine with FTS (type, status, priority, labels)
- [ ] Pagination support (limit, offset)
- [ ] Snippet generation with term highlighting
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output formats
- [ ] rebuild_fts_index() for repair/initial population
- [ ] Handle invalid FTS5 syntax gracefully with clear error message

## Unit Tests
- Test basic term search
- Test phrase search ("exact phrase")
- Test prefix search (term*)
- Test boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT)
- Test column filters (title:term)
- Test filter combinations with FTS
- Test BM25 ranking (more relevant results first)
- Test snippet generation
- Test empty results
- Test invalid FTS syntax error handling
- Test FTS index stays in sync after CRUD operations

## Dependencies
- Requires Database Schema & Migrations (FTS5 table creation)
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer Core
- Requires Model Types (IssueWithScore struct)

## Performance Considerations
- FTS5 queries are O(log n) not O(n)
- Index size approximately 2-3x text size
- Consider VACUUM after bulk imports
- Use LIMIT to avoid returning huge result sets

## Rationale
Full-text search is essential for discovering issues in large projects. FTS5 provides fast, relevance-ranked search with minimal code. This enables workflows like "find all issues mentioning authentication" which would otherwise require reading through hundreds of issues.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:10.810505346Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.787829331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:50:01.787829331Z","close_reason":"Superseded by beads_rust-aww (LIKE-based search for classic bd parity)"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-egz8","title":"Test Harness Foundation Enhancements","description":"# Test Harness Foundation Enhancements

## Purpose
Before expanding conformance tests, the existing test harness needs significant enhancements to support:
- **Comprehensive logging** with multiple levels and structured output
- **More comparison modes** (array ordering tolerance, field exclusion)
- **Better error diagnostics** (show detailed diff on failure with actionable suggestions)
- **Benchmark timing infrastructure** (warmup, multiple runs, statistics)
- **Shared test scenarios** that can be reused for benchmarks
- **Test report generation** (JUnit XML, summary reports)

## Current State
The existing `tests/conformance.rs` has:
- `ConformanceWorkspace` struct for paired br/bd directories
- `run_br()` and `run_bd()` methods with basic file logging
- `CompareMode` enum with 4 modes
- `normalize_json()` for timestamp/ID masking
- Basic logging to files (timestamp, duration, stdout/stderr)

## Required Enhancements

### 1. Comprehensive Logging Infrastructure

**Log Levels**:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Error = 0,   // Only failures and errors
    Warn = 1,    // + warnings (e.g., slow tests)
    Info = 2,    // + test progress, summaries
    Debug = 3,   // + command details, timings
    Trace = 4,   // + raw outputs, internal state
}
```

**Structured Log Format**:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub test_name: String,
    pub phase: TestPhase,  // Setup, Execute, Compare, Teardown
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
pub enum TestPhase {
    Setup,
    ExecuteBr,
    ExecuteBd,
    Compare,
    Teardown,
}
```

**Log Destinations**:
```rust
pub struct LogConfig {
    pub console_level: LogLevel,      // What to print to stderr
    pub file_level: LogLevel,         // What to write to log files
    pub structured_json: bool,        // JSON vs human-readable
    pub log_dir: PathBuf,
    pub aggregate_log: PathBuf,       // Single file with all tests
    pub per_test_logs: bool,          // Individual log per test
}
```

**Usage in tests**:
```rust
fn conformance_create_basic() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("conformance_create_basic");
    
    logger.info("Setting up workspace");
    let workspace = ConformanceWorkspace::new();
    
    logger.debug("Initializing both workspaces");
    workspace.init_both();
    
    logger.info("Executing br create");
    let br_result = workspace.run_br(["create", "Test", "--json"], "create");
    logger.trace("br output", json!({"stdout": br_result.stdout, "stderr": br_result.stderr}));
    
    logger.info("Executing bd create");
    let bd_result = workspace.run_bd(["create", "Test", "--json"], "create");
    logger.trace("bd output", json!({"stdout": bd_result.stdout, "stderr": bd_result.stderr}));
    
    logger.info("Comparing outputs");
    let comparison = compare_json(&br_result.stdout, &bd_result.stdout, &mode);
    
    if let Err(e) = &comparison {
        logger.error("Comparison failed", json!({"error": e}));
    }
    
    logger.summary(TestResult { passed: comparison.is_ok(), ... });
}
```

### 2. Enhanced Comparison Modes
```rust
pub enum CompareMode {
    // Existing
    ExactJson,
    NormalizedJson,
    ContainsFields(Vec<String>),
    ExitCodeOnly,
    
    // NEW
    ArrayUnordered,              // Compare arrays ignoring order
    FieldsExcluded(Vec<String>), // Ignore specified fields
    StructureOnly,               // Compare JSON structure, not values
    RegexMatch(String),          // Output matches regex pattern
    CustomComparator(Box<dyn Fn(&Value, &Value) -> Result<(), String>>),
}
```

### 3. Detailed Diff on Failure
```rust
pub struct ComparisonFailure {
    pub mode: CompareMode,
    pub br_output: String,
    pub bd_output: String,
    pub diff: String,           // Human-readable diff
    pub diff_html: String,      // HTML diff for CI artifacts
    pub suggestion: Option<String>,  // Actionable fix suggestion
    pub context: DiffContext,
}

pub struct DiffContext {
    pub br_exit_code: i32,
    pub bd_exit_code: i32,
    pub br_duration: Duration,
    pub bd_duration: Duration,
    pub normalized_br: Option<Value>,
    pub normalized_bd: Option<Value>,
}

fn diff_json(br: &Value, bd: &Value) -> ComparisonFailure {
    // Generate colored, line-by-line diff
    // Identify specific fields that differ
    // Suggest potential fixes based on common patterns
}
```

**Diff output example**:

br output (normalized):

### 4. Benchmark Timing Infrastructure
```rust
pub struct BenchmarkConfig {
    pub warmup_runs: usize,      // Default: 3
    pub timed_runs: usize,       // Default: 10
    pub outlier_threshold: f64,  // Remove runs outside N std devs
    pub timeout: Duration,       // Max time per run
}

pub struct TimingStats {
    pub samples: Vec<Duration>,
    pub mean: Duration,
    pub median: Duration,
    pub p95: Duration,
    pub p99: Duration,
    pub std_dev: Duration,
    pub min: Duration,
    pub max: Duration,
    pub outliers_removed: usize,
}

impl BenchmarkConfig {
    pub fn run_timed<F, R>(&self, f: F) -> TimingStats
    where
        F: Fn() -> R,
    {
        // Warmup runs (discard)
        for _ in 0..self.warmup_runs {
            let _ = f();
        }
        
        // Timed runs
        let mut samples = Vec::with_capacity(self.timed_runs);
        for _ in 0..self.timed_runs {
            let start = Instant::now();
            let _ = f();
            samples.push(start.elapsed());
        }
        
        TimingStats::from_samples(samples, self.outlier_threshold)
    }
}
```

## Test Logging Requirements

Every test MUST use structured logging:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_example() {
    init_test_logging();  // REQUIRED
    info!("Starting test_example");

    // Log setup
    debug!(param = ?value, "Setting up test data");

    // Log action
    info!(action = "create_issue", id = %issue.id, "Performing action");

    // Log verification
    debug!(expected = ?expected, actual = ?actual, "Verifying result");

    // Assertions with context
    assert_eq!(actual, expected, "Issue count mismatch after operation");

    info!("test_example completed successfully");
}
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-jjs","title":"create --file markdown bulk import","description":"# create --file (Markdown bulk create)

## Purpose
Support classic `bd create --file <markdown>` behavior for bulk issue creation. This is **not** JSONL import; it is a CLI convenience with a specific Markdown grammar and known quirks.

## CLI
```
br create --file <path> [flags]
```
Constraints:
- `--file` is mutually exclusive with positional title.
- `--dry-run` is **not** supported with `--file` (must error).
- File must be `.md` or `.markdown`, must exist, **must not** contain `..`.

## Markdown Grammar
- Each issue starts with an **H2** line:
  - `## Issue Title`
- Per-issue sections are **H3** lines:
  - `### Section Name`
- Recognized sections (case-insensitive):
  - `Priority`, `Type`, `Description`, `Design`, `Acceptance Criteria` (alias `Acceptance`),
    `Assignee`, `Labels`, `Dependencies` (alias `Deps`).
- Unknown sections are ignored.

### Known Quirk (Must Match bd)
- Lines immediately after the H2 title **before any H3** are treated as description,
  but **only the first non-empty line** is captured; subsequent lines are ignored.

## Parsing Rules
- Section content captured verbatim until next H2/H3.
- Labels/deps split on commas **or** whitespace.
- Dependencies accept `type:id` or bare `id` (default `blocks`).
- Invalid dependency types are **warned and skipped**.

## Creation Behavior
- Direct mode: create sequentially; add labels/deps after create; warnings are non-fatal.
- Daemon is excluded in br; use direct storage only.

## Output
- JSON mode: array of created Issue objects (successes only).
- Human mode:
  - `✓ Created N issues from <file>:` and per-issue summary lines.
  - Failures are printed to stderr as `✗ Failed to create ...`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Grammar and quirks match legacy behavior exactly.
- Invalid deps/labels warn but do not abort batch.
- JSON output shape matches bd.

## Tests
- Parse fixture markdown with multiple issues and sections.
- Verify description quirk (only first non-empty line captured).
- Verify label/dep splitting and invalid-type warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlackBeaver","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:01.915794871Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:15:41.632440202-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:15:41.632440202-05:00","close_reason":"Verified create --file markdown import implementation + tests pass"}

- Output is ID-only regardless of `--json`.
- Mirrors bd quick capture behavior.

 {"id":"beads_rust-k0y","title":"doctor Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br doctor` command to diagnose project health issues and suggest fixes. This is essential for troubleshooting corrupted databases, orphaned references, and configuration problems.

## CLI Interface
```
br doctor [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --fix                     Attempt to automatically fix issues
  --check <CHECK>           Run specific check only (can repeat)
  --skip <CHECK>            Skip specific check (can repeat)
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output

Available checks:
  schema       - Database schema integrity
  refs         - Referential integrity (deps, labels)
  blocked      - Blocked cache consistency
  fts          - FTS5 index consistency
  orphans      - Orphaned dependencies/labels
  config       - Configuration validity
  jsonl        - JSONL file consistency
```

## Technical Requirements

### Health Check Framework
```rust
pub trait HealthCheck: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn description(&self) -> &str;
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult>;
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult>;
}

pub enum CheckResult {
    Ok,
    Warning(String),
    Error(String),
}

pub enum FixResult {
    Fixed(String),
    CannotFix(String),
    NoFixNeeded,
}
```

### Individual Checks

#### Schema Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for SchemaCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Verify all expected tables exist
        let expected = ["issues", "dependencies", "labels", "comments", 
                        "events", "config", "metadata", "dirty_issues",
                        "blocked_issues_cache", "child_counters"];
        for table in expected {
            if !storage.table_exists(table)? {
                return Ok(CheckResult::Error(format!("Missing table: {}", table)));
            }
        }
        // Verify pragma values
        let journal = storage.pragma_query_value("journal_mode")?;
        if journal != "wal" {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Warning("journal_mode not WAL".into()));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
}
```

#### Referential Integrity Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for RefIntegrityCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Find dependencies pointing to non-existent issues
        let orphans = storage.query::<String>(
            "SELECT d.issue_id FROM dependencies d 
             LEFT JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on_id = i.id 
             WHERE i.id IS NULL"
        )?;
        if !orphans.is_empty() {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error(
                format!("{} orphaned dependencies found", orphans.len())
            ));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        let deleted = storage.execute(
            "DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE depends_on_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM issues)"
        )?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed(format!("Deleted {} orphaned dependencies", deleted)))
    }
}
```

#### Blocked Cache Consistency
```rust
impl HealthCheck for BlockedCacheCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Recompute blocked issues and compare with cache
        let computed = storage.compute_blocked_issues()?;
        let cached = storage.get_blocked_from_cache()?;
        if computed != cached {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error("Blocked cache out of sync".into()));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        storage.rebuild_blocked_issues_cache()?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed("Rebuilt blocked issues cache".into()))
    }
}
```

#### FTS5 Index Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for FtsCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        let sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM issues_fts)";
        let missing = storage.query_single::<i64>(sql)?;
        if missing > 0 {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error(format!("{} issues missing from FTS", missing)));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        storage.rebuild_fts_index()?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed("Rebuilt FTS index".into()))
    }
}
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Running health checks...

✓ schema        Database schema is valid
✓ refs          Referential integrity OK
⚠ blocked       Blocked cache has 3 stale entries (--fix to repair)
✓ fts           FTS5 index consistent
✓ config        Configuration valid
✓ jsonl         JSONL files consistent

Summary: 5 passed, 1 warning, 0 errors
Run with --fix to repair warnings automatically
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "checks": [
    { "name": "schema", "status": "ok" },
    { "name": "blocked", "status": "warning", "message": "3 stale entries" }
  ],
  "summary": { "passed": 5, "warnings": 1, "errors": 0 },
  "fixable": true
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run all checks by default
- [ ] Schema integrity check
- [ ] Referential integrity check (deps, labels point to valid issues)
- [ ] Blocked cache consistency check
- [ ] FTS5 index consistency check
- [ ] Configuration validity check
- [ ] JSONL sync status check
- [ ] --fix flag to auto-repair
- [ ] --check to run specific checks
- [ ] --skip to exclude checks
- [ ] Human and JSON output

## Unit Tests
- All checks pass on healthy database
- Schema check detects missing table
- Ref check detects orphaned dependency
- Blocked cache check detects stale entry
- FTS check detects missing issue
- Fix flag repairs issues
- Multiple checks can be selected/skipped

## Dependencies
- SQLite Storage Layer Core
- JSONL Export/Import (for JSONL check)
- Blocked Cache Rebuild

## Rationale
The doctor command is essential for troubleshooting. Users can run it when they suspect corruption or after problematic operations. Auto-fix capability reduces manual intervention.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:34.411206677Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:36:08.56578283Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:36:08.56578283Z","close_reason":"Superseded by minimal doctor bead (beads_rust-2hr)"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-rdrp","title":"BUG: NOT NULL constraint fails on optional fields during insert","description":"# BUG: NOT NULL constraint fails on optional fields during insert

## Symptom
All E2E tests fail with:
```
Database error: NOT NULL constraint failed: issues.description
```

## Root Cause
Schema has: `description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`
But INSERT statement passes `issue.description` directly as `Option<String>`.
When `None`, SQLite receives NULL which violates NOT NULL constraint.

## Fix
Convert `None` to empty string before INSERT for all `NOT NULL DEFAULT ''` fields:
- description
- design
- acceptance_criteria
- notes
- owner
- created_by
- close_reason
- closed_by_session
- source_system
- deleted_by
- delete_reason
- original_type
- sender

## Location
src/storage/sqlite.rs lines 193-230 (insert_issue function)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:39:37.812410166-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:44.918596194-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:44.918596194-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed NOT NULL constraint bug: update_issue now converts None to empty string for NOT NULL DEFAULT '' fields, and issue_from_row converts empty strings back to None for API consistency.
